text: This chapter introduces the language of sets , a convenient way to reason about the objects in a type which have some property. This language includes the concept of membership in a set, the property of a set being a subset of another set, and a whole zoo of set operations, such as intersection , union and complement , each of which is a wrapper for a logical symbol relating the underlying properties.
html: This chapter introduces the language of sets, a convenient way to reason about the objects in a type which have some property. This language includes the concept of membership in a set, the property of a set being a subset of another set, and a whole zoo of set operations, such as intersection, union and complement, each of which is a wrapper for a logical symbol relating the underlying properties.
zh: 本章介绍集合语言。集合语言是一种方便的方式，用来讨论某个类型中具有某种性质的对象。这个语言包括属于某集合的概念、一个集合是另一个集合的子集这一性质，以及一整套集合运算，例如交、并和补；其中每一种运算都可以看作是对底层性质之间某个逻辑符号的封装。

text: In the last section of the chapter, Section 9.3 , we study the collection of sets in a type as a type in its own right.
html: In the last section of the chapter, Section 9.3, we study the collection of sets in a type as a type in its own right.
zh: 在本章最后一节，即第 9.3 节中，我们把某个类型中的所有集合组成的全体本身也作为一种类型来研究。

text: In type theory, the logical foundation for this book, a set in a type \(X\) is specified by a predicate on \(X\) . For example, “the set of integers \(n\) such that \(n\le 3\) ” is a set in \(\mathbb{Z}\) .
html: In type theory, the logical foundation for this book, a set in a type \(X\) is specified by a predicate on \(X\). For example, “the set of integers \(n\) such that \(n\le 3\)” is a set in \(\mathbb{Z}\).
zh: 在类型论，即本书采用的逻辑基础中，类型 \(X\) 中的一个集合由 \(X\) 上的一个谓词指定。例如，“满足 \(n\le 3\) 的整数 \(n\) 的集合”就是 \(\mathbb{Z}\) 中的一个集合。

text: There is a standard notation for sets specified by predicates. For example, the set described above is written notationally as \(\{n:\mathbb{Z} \mid n\le 3\}\) . Here is that set in Lean.
html: There is a standard notation for sets specified by predicates. For example, the set described above is written notationally as \(\{n:\mathbb{Z} \mid n\le 3\}\). Here is that set in Lean.

text: Note that the infoview confirms that the type of the expression is Set ℤ , a set of integers.
html: Note that the infoview confirms that the type of the expression is Set ℤ, a set of integers.
zh: 注意，infoview 确认该表达式的类型为 Set ℤ，即整数集合。

text: A term of type \(X\) belongs to a set in \(X\) specified by some predicate, if the predicate holds for that term.
html: A term of type \(X\) belongs to a set in \(X\) specified by some predicate, if the predicate holds for that term.
zh: 类型 \(X\) 的一个项属于 \(X\) 中由某个谓词指定的集合，当且仅当该谓词对这个项成立。

html: Conversely, suppose that there exists an integer \(k\) such that \(x=2k-1\). Then
zh: 反过来，假设存在整数 \(k\) 使得 \(x=2k-1\)。则

text: Problem
html: Problem

text: Show that \(\{a:\mathbb{N} \mid 4\mid a\}\ne\{b:\mathbb{N} \mid 2\mid b\}\) .
html: Show that \(\{a:\mathbb{N} \mid 4\mid a\}\ne\{b:\mathbb{N} \mid 2\mid b\}\).

text: Solution
html: Solution

text: We will show that there exists a natural number \(x\) such that \(2\mid x\) and \(4\not\mid 6\) .
html: We will show that there exists a natural number \(x\) such that \(2\mid x\) and \(4\not\mid 6\).
zh: 我们将证明存在自然数 \(x\)，使得 \(2\mid x\) 且 \(4\not\mid x\)。

text: Indeed, let us show that \(6\) has this property. We have that \(6=2\cdot 3\) , so \(2\mid 6\) , and \(4\cdot 1 < 6 < 4 \cdot 2\) , so \(4\not\mid 6\) .
html: Indeed, let us show that \(6\) has this property. We have that \(6=2\cdot 3\), so \(2\mid 6\), and \(4\cdot 1 < 6 < 4 \cdot 2\), so \(4\not\mid 6\).
zh: 确实，我们证明 \(6\) 具有这个性质。因为 \(6=2\cdot 3\)，所以 \(2\mid 6\)；并且 \(4\cdot 1 < 6 < 4\cdot 2\)，所以 \(4\not\mid 6\)。

text: Let \(n\) be an integer. We will show that \(5n\) is a multiple of 8 if and only if \(n\) is.

text: so \(8\mid n\) .

text: Conversely, suppose that \(8\mid n\) . Then there exists an integer \(a\) such that \(n=8a\) . So
html: Conversely, suppose that \(8\mid n\). Then there exists an integer \(a\) such that \(n=8a\). So

text: Definition

text: Problem

text: We will show that for all real numbers \(t\) , if \(t\) is -2 or 3 and \(t^2=9\) then \(0<t\) .

text: Indeed, let \(t\) be a real number and suppose that \(t\) is -2 or 3 and \(t^2=9\) .
html: Indeed, let \(t\) be a real number and suppose that \(t\) is -2 or 3 and \(t^2=9\).
zh: 确实，设 \(t\) 为实数，并假设 \(t\) 为 -2 或 3 且 \(t^2=9\)。

text: Case 1 ( \(t=-2\) ): We then have that
html: Case 1 (\(t=-2\)): We then have that

text: contradiction.

text: Case 2 ( \(t=3\) ): Then \(0<t\) as desired.
html: Case 2 (\(t=3\)): Then \(0<t\) as desired.

text: Problem
html: Problem

text: Show that \(\{n:\mathbb{N}\mid 4\le n\}\cap \{n:\mathbb{N}\mid n<7\}\subseteq\{4,5,6\}\) .
html: Show that \(\{n:\mathbb{N}\mid 4\le n\}\cap \{n:\mathbb{N}\mid n<7\}\subseteq\{4,5,6\}\).

text: Definition
html: Definition

text: The complement of a set \(U\) in a type \(X\) , denoted \(U^{c}\) , is \(\{x : X \mid x \notin U\}\) .
html: The complement of a set \(U\) in a type \(X\), denoted \(U^{c}\), is \(\{x : X \mid x \notin U\}\).

text: Problem
html: Problem

text: Show that \(\{n:\mathbb{Z}\mid n\text{ even}\}^{c}=\{n:\mathbb{N}\mid n\text{ odd}\}\) .
html: Show that \(\{n:\mathbb{Z}\mid n\text{ even}\}^{c}=\{n:\mathbb{N}\mid n\text{ odd}\}\).
zh: 证明 \(\{n:\mathbb{Z}\mid n\text{ even}\}^{c}=\{n:\mathbb{N}\mid n\text{ odd}\}\)。

text: Solution
html: Solution

text: Let \(n\) be an integer. We must show that \(n\) is odd if and only if it is not even. This is precisely Example 4.5.5 .
html: Let \(n\) be an integer. We must show that \(n\) is odd if and only if it is not even. This is precisely Example 4.5.5.
zh: 设 \(n\) 为整数。我们必须证明 \(n\) 为奇数，当且仅当它不是偶数。这正是例 4.5.5。

text: The empty set in a type \(X\) is the set which has no elements. That’s a slightly informal description: here’s the strict definition.
html: The empty set in a type \(X\) is the set which has no elements. That’s a slightly informal description: here’s the strict definition.
zh: 类型 \(X\) 中的空集是不含任何元素的集合。这是稍微非形式化的描述；下面是严格定义。

text: Definition
html: Definition

text: The empty set in a type \(X\) , denoted \(\emptyset\) , is \(\{x : X \mid \operatorname{False}\}\) .
html: The empty set in a type \(X\), denoted \(\emptyset\), is \(\{x : X \mid \operatorname{False}\}\).
zh: 类型 \(X\) 中的空集，记为 \(\emptyset\)，定义为 \(\{x : X \mid \operatorname{False}\}\)。

text: It is true by pure logic that no element of type \(X\) belongs to the empty set in \(X\) , and that the empty set in \(X\) is a subset of every set in \(X\) .
html: It is true by pure logic that no element of type \(X\) belongs to the empty set in \(X\), and that the empty set in \(X\) is a subset of every set in \(X\).
zh: 纯逻辑即可说明：类型 \(X\) 的任何元素都不属于 \(X\) 中的空集，并且 \(X\) 中的空集是 \(X\) 中每个集合的子集。

text: To show a set \(U\) in \(X\) is equal to the empty set, you must show that \(U\) has no elements.
html: To show a set \(U\) in \(X\) is equal to the empty set, you must show that \(U\) has no elements.
zh: 要证明 \(X\) 中的集合 \(U\) 等于空集，你必须证明 \(U\) 没有元素。

text: Let \(x\) be an integer. We will show that it is not true that both \(x\equiv 1\mod 5\) and \(x\equiv 2\mod 5\) .
html: Let \(x\) be an integer. We will show that it is not true that both \(x\equiv 1\mod 5\) and \(x\equiv 2\mod 5\).

text: The universe set in a type \(X\) is the set which contains everything in \(X\) . That’s again a slightly informal description: here’s the strict definition.
html: The universe set in a type \(X\) is the set which contains everything in \(X\). That’s again a slightly informal description: here’s the strict definition.
zh: 类型 \(X\) 中的全集是包含 \(X\) 中所有对象的集合。这同样是稍微非形式化的描述；下面是严格定义。

text: Definition
html: Definition

text: The universe set in a type \(X\) is \(\{x : X \mid \operatorname{True}\}\) .
html: The universe set in a type \(X\) is \(\{x : X \mid \operatorname{True}\}\).
zh: 类型 \(X\) 中的全集是 \(\{x : X \mid \operatorname{True}\}\)。

text: It is true by pure logic that all elements of type \(X\) belong to the universe set in \(X\) , and that every set in \(X\) is a subset of the universe set in \(X\) .
html: It is true by pure logic that all elements of type \(X\) belong to the universe set in \(X\), and that every set in \(X\) is a subset of the universe set in \(X\).
zh: 纯逻辑即可说明：类型 \(X\) 的所有元素都属于 \(X\) 中的全集，并且 \(X\) 中每个集合都是 \(X\) 中全集的子集。

text: Note that in Lean the universe set is denoted univ . On paper we often refer to the universe set of \(X\) as \(X\) , too (even though this is not strictly correct).
html: Note that in Lean the universe set is denoted univ. On paper we often refer to the universe set of \(X\) as \(X\), too (even though this is not strictly correct).
zh: 注意，在 Lean 中全集记为 univ。在纸上，我们也常把 \(X\) 的全集称为 \(X\) 本身（尽管严格说来这并不正确）。

text: To show a set \(U\) in \(X\) is equal to the universe set, you must show that \(U\) contains all elements of \(X\) . Here we adapt Example 9.2.1 to be a problem about the universe set.
html: To show a set \(U\) in \(X\) is equal to the universe set, you must show that \(U\) contains all elements of \(X\). Here we adapt Example 9.2.1 to be a problem about the universe set.
zh: 要证明 \(X\) 中的集合 \(U\) 等于全集，你必须证明 \(U\) 包含 \(X\) 的所有元素。这里我们把例 9.2.1 改写成一个关于全集的问题。

text: Problem
html: Problem

text: Show that \(\{x:\mathbb{R}\mid-1<x\}\cup \{x:\mathbb{R}\mid x < 1\}=\mathbb{R}\) .
html: Show that \(\{x:\mathbb{R}\mid-1<x\}\cup \{x:\mathbb{R}\mid x < 1\}=\mathbb{R}\).

text: Solution
html: Solution

text: We must show that for all real numbers \(t\) , either \(-1<t\) or \(t<1\) .
html: We must show that for all real numbers \(t\), either \(-1<t\) or \(t<1\).
zh: 我们必须证明对所有实数 \(t\)，都有 \(-1<t\) 或 \(t<1\)。

text: Case 1 ( \(t\le 0\) ): Then \(t<1\) .
html: Case 1 (\(t\le 0\)): Then \(t<1\).
Case 2 (\(t> 0\)): Then \(-1<t\).

text: For the first five problems, I provide a tactic check_equality_of_explicit_sets which will prove the statement if you have formulated it correctly. This tactic simply runs ext , then dsimp , then exhaust :
html: For the first five problems, I provide a tactic check_equality_of_explicit_sets which will prove the statement if you have formulated it correctly. This tactic simply runs ext, then dsimp, then exhaust:
zh: 对于前五个问题，我提供了策略 check_equality_of_explicit_sets；只要你把陈述表述正确，它就会证明该陈述。这个策略只是依次运行 ext、dsimp、exhaust：

text: Let \(X\) be a type. The collection of all sets in \(X\) can itself be considered as a type. This type is sometimes denoted \(\mathcal{P}(X)\) . 1 For example, \(\{3,4,5\}\) , \(\{n:\mathbb{N}\mid 8<n\}\) , and \(\{k:\mathbb{N}\mid\exists \ a, a^2=k\}\) are all sets of natural numbers, which means they are all of type \(\mathcal{P}(\mathbb{N})\) .
html: Let \(X\) be a type. The collection of all sets in \(X\) can itself be considered as a type. This type is sometimes denoted \(\mathcal{P}(X)\). 1 For example, \(\{3,4,5\}\), \(\{n:\mathbb{N}\mid 8<n\}\), and \(\{k:\mathbb{N}\mid\exists \ a, a^2=k\}\) are all sets of natural numbers, which means they are all of type \(\mathcal{P}(\mathbb{N})\).
zh: 设 \(X\) 为一个类型。\(X\) 中所有集合组成的全体本身也可以看作一种类型。这个类型有时记为 \(\mathcal{P}(X)\)。例如，\(\{3,4,5\}\)、\(\{n:\mathbb{N}\mid 8<n\}\) 和 \(\{k:\mathbb{N}\mid\exists a, a^2=k\}\) 都是自然数集合，这意味着它们都具有类型 \(\mathcal{P}(\mathbb{N})\)。

text: In Lean, for a type X , the type of sets in X is denoted Set X . Lean will confirm that the three objects described above all have type Set ℕ :
html: In Lean, for a type X, the type of sets in X is denoted Set X. Lean will confirm that the three objects described above all have type Set ℕ:
zh: 在 Lean 中，对于类型 \(X\)，\(X\) 中集合的类型记为 Set X。Lean 会确认上面描述的三个对象都具有类型 Set ℕ：

text: This operation can be iterated: you can have sets in the type of sets, and so on.
html: This operation can be iterated: you can have sets in the type of sets, and so on.
zh: 这个操作可以迭代：你可以考虑以集合类型为底层类型的集合，如此继续。

text: Exercise: write down an object of type Set (Set (Set ℕ)) .
html: Exercise: write down an object of type Set (Set (Set ℕ)).
zh: 练习：写出一个类型为 Set (Set (Set ℕ)) 的对象。

text: Problem
html: Problem

text: Show that \(\{n:\mathbb{N}\mid n\text{ is even}\}\notin\{s:\mathcal{P}(\mathbb{N})\mid 3 ∈s\}\) .
html: Show that \(\{n:\mathbb{N}\mid n\text{ is even}\}\notin\{s:\mathcal{P}(\mathbb{N})\mid 3 ∈s\}\).

text: Solution
html: Solution

text: We must show 3 is not even. It suffices to show that 3 is odd. Indeed, \(3=2\cdot 1+1\) .
html: We must show 3 is not even. It suffices to show that 3 is odd. Indeed, \(3=2\cdot 1+1\).
zh: 我们必须证明 3 不是偶数。只需证明 3 为奇数。确实，\(3=2\cdot 1+1\)。

text: Since \(\mathcal{P}(X)\) , the type of sets in \(X\) , is itself a type, we can consider functions to and from it.
html: Since \(\mathcal{P}(X)\), the type of sets in \(X\), is itself a type, we can consider functions to and from it.
zh: 由于 \(\mathcal{P}(X)\)，即 \(X\) 中集合的类型，本身也是一个类型，我们可以考虑以它为定义域或陪域的函数。

text: For example, given a set \(s\) of natural numbers, we can build a new set \(\{n:\mathbb{N}\mid n+1 \in s\}\) . Lean confirms that this operation (let us call it \(p\) ) is a function from \(\mathcal{P}(\mathbb{N})\) to \(\mathcal{P}(\mathbb{N})\) .
html: For example, given a set \(s\) of natural numbers, we can build a new set \(\{n:\mathbb{N}\mid n+1 \in s\}\). Lean confirms that this operation (let us call it \(p\)) is a function from \(\mathcal{P}(\mathbb{N})\) to \(\mathcal{P}(\mathbb{N})\).
zh: 例如，给定一个自然数集合 \(s\)，可以构造新集合 \(\{n:\mathbb{N}\mid n+1 \in s\}\)。Lean 确认这一操作（我们称之为 \(p\)）是从 \(\mathcal{P}(\mathbb{N})\) 到 \(\mathcal{P}(\mathbb{N})\) 的函数。

text: Problem
html: Problem

text: Show that the function \(p\) is not injective.
html: Show that the function \(p\) is not injective.
zh: 证明函数 \(p\) 不是单射。

text: Solution
html: Solution

text: We must show that there exist sets \(s\) and \(t\) , such that (i) \(\{n:\mathbb{N}\mid n+1 \in s\} = \{n:\mathbb{N}\mid n+1 \in t\}\) , and (ii) \(s \ne t\) .
html: We must show that there exist sets \(s\) and \(t\), such that (i) \(\{n:\mathbb{N}\mid n+1 \in s\} = \{n:\mathbb{N}\mid n+1 \in t\}\), and (ii) \(s \ne t\).
zh: 我们必须证明存在集合 \(s\) 和 \(t\)，使得 (i) \(\{n:\mathbb{N}\mid n+1 \in s\} = \{n:\mathbb{N}\mid n+1 \in t\}\)，并且 (ii) \(s\ne t\)。

text: Indeed, let us show that the sets \(\{0\}\) and \(\emptyset\) have this property. We must show, (i) \(\{n:\mathbb{N}\mid n+1 = 0\} = \emptyset\) , and (ii) \(\{0\} \ne \emptyset\) .
html: Indeed, let us show that the sets \(\{0\}\) and \(\emptyset\) have this property. We must show, (i) \(\{n:\mathbb{N}\mid n+1 = 0\} = \emptyset\), and (ii) \(\{0\} \ne \emptyset\).
zh: 确实，我们证明集合 \(\{0\}\) 与 \(\emptyset\) 具有这个性质。我们必须证明：(i) \(\{n:\mathbb{N}\mid n+1 = 0\} = \emptyset\)，以及 (ii) \(\{0\} \ne \emptyset\)。

text: For the first statement, let \(x\) be a natural number. We must show that \(x+1 \ne 0\) , which is true since \(x+1 > 0\) .
html: For the first statement, let \(x\) be a natural number. We must show that \(x+1 \ne 0\), which is true since \(x+1 > 0\).
zh: 对于第一个陈述，设 \(x\) 为自然数。我们必须证明 \(x+1\ne 0\)，这是因为 \(x+1>0\)。

text: For the second statement, we must show that there exists a natural number \(k\) such that \(k\in\{0\}\) and \(k\notin\emptyset\) , or vice versa. Indeed, \(k=0\) has this property.
html: For the second statement, we must show that there exists a natural number \(k\) such that \(k\in\{0\}\) and \(k\notin\emptyset\), or vice versa. Indeed, \(k=0\) has this property.
zh: 对于第二个陈述，我们必须证明存在自然数 \(k\)，使得 \(k\in\{0\}\) 且 \(k\notin\emptyset\)，或反过来。确实，\(k=0\) 具有这个性质。

text: Problem
html: Problem

text: Consider the function \(q : \mathcal{P}(\mathbb{Z})\to \mathcal{P}(\mathbb{Z})\) defined by, \(q(s)=\{n:\mathbb{Z}\mid n+1\in s\}\) . Show that the function \(q\) is injective.
html: Consider the function \(q : \mathcal{P}(\mathbb{Z})\to \mathcal{P}(\mathbb{Z})\) defined by, \(q(s)=\{n:\mathbb{Z}\mid n+1\in s\}\). Show that the function \(q\) is injective.
zh: 考虑函数 \(q : \mathcal{P}(\mathbb{Z})\to \mathcal{P}(\mathbb{Z})\)，定义为 \(q(s)=\{n:\mathbb{Z}\mid n+1\in s\}\)。证明函数 \(q\) 是单射。

text: Solution
html: Solution

text: We must show that for all sets \(s\) and \(t\) , if \(\{n:\mathbb{Z}\mid n+1 \in s\} = \{n:\mathbb{Z}\mid n+1 \in t\}\) , then \(s = t\) .
html: We must show that for all sets \(s\) and \(t\), if \(\{n:\mathbb{Z}\mid n+1 \in s\} = \{n:\mathbb{Z}\mid n+1 \in t\}\), then \(s = t\).
zh: 我们必须证明对所有集合 \(s\) 和 \(t\)，若 \(\{n:\mathbb{Z}\mid n+1 \in s\} = \{n:\mathbb{Z}\mid n+1 \in t\}\)，则 \(s=t\)。

text: Indeed, let \(s\) and \(t\) be sets and suppose that \(\{n:\mathbb{Z}\mid n+1 \in s\} = \{n:\mathbb{Z}\mid n+1 \in t\}\) .
html: Indeed, let \(s\) and \(t\) be sets and suppose that \(\{n:\mathbb{Z}\mid n+1 \in s\} = \{n:\mathbb{Z}\mid n+1 \in t\}\).
zh: 确实，设 \(s\) 和 \(t\) 为集合，并假设 \(\{n:\mathbb{Z}\mid n+1 \in s\} = \{n:\mathbb{Z}\mid n+1 \in t\}\)。

text: Let \(k\) be an integer. We must show that \(k\in s\) if and only if \(k\in t\) . Indeed, by the assumption, \(k -1\in\{n:\mathbb{Z}\mid n+1 \in s\}\) if and only if \(k-1\in \{n:\mathbb{Z}\mid n+1 \in t\}\) . Simplifying, \(k -1+1 \in s\) if and only if \(k-1+1\in t\) , and so \(k \in s\) if and only if \(k\in t\) .
html: Let \(k\) be an integer. We must show that \(k\in s\) if and only if \(k\in t\). Indeed, by the assumption, \(k -1\in\{n:\mathbb{Z}\mid n+1 \in s\}\) if and only if \(k-1\in \{n:\mathbb{Z}\mid n+1 \in t\}\). Simplifying, \(k -1+1 \in s\) if and only if \(k-1+1\in t\), and so \(k \in s\) if and only if \(k\in t\).
zh: 设 \(k\) 为整数。我们必须证明 \(k\in s\) 当且仅当 \(k\in t\)。确实，由假设可知，\(k-1\in\{n:\mathbb{Z}\mid n+1\in s\}\) 当且仅当 \(k-1\in\{n:\mathbb{Z}\mid n+1\in t\}\)。化简后，\(k-1+1\in s\) 当且仅当 \(k-1+1\in t\)，因此 \(k\in s\) 当且仅当 \(k\in t\)。

text: Problem
html: Problem

text: Let \(X\) be a type. Prove that there does not exist a surjective function from \(X\) to \(\mathcal{P}(X)\) .
html: Let \(X\) be a type. Prove that there does not exist a surjective function from \(X\) to \(\mathcal{P}(X)\).
zh: 设 \(X\) 为一个类型。证明不存在从 \(X\) 到 \(\mathcal{P}(X)\) 的满射函数。

html: Solution

text: Suppose for the sake of contradiction that some function \(f:X\to\mathcal{P}(X)\) were surjective. We introduce the following set in \(X\) :

html: Since \(f\) is surjective, there exists some \(x\) of type \(X\) such that \(f(x)=s\). We consider cases according to whether \(x\in s\).

html: Case 1 (\(x\in s\)): Then by the definition of \(s\), it is true that \(x\notin f(x)\), so since \(f(x)=s\), we have \(x\notin s\), contradiction.

text: Let us define that a set \(s\) in a type \(X\) has size at least two , if there exist \(x_1\) and \(x_2\) in \(s\) which are different, and has size at least three , if there exist \(x_1\) , \(x_2\) and \(x_3\) in \(s\) which are all different. Let \(s\) and \(t\) be sets of size at least two in some type \(X\) , and suppose that \(s \cap t\) does not have size at least two. Show that \(s\cup t\) has size at least three. This problem features a ton of different cases. Use exhaust liberally to knock off sub-cases. def SizeAtLeastTwo ( s : Set X ) : Prop := ∃ x1 x2 : X , x1 ≠ x2 ∧ x1 ∈ s ∧ x2 ∈ s def SizeAtLeastThree ( s : Set X ) : Prop := ∃ x1 x2 x3 : X , x1 ≠ x2 ∧ x1 ≠ x3 ∧ x2 ≠ x3 ∧ x1 ∈ s ∧ x2 ∈ s ∧ x3 ∈ s example { s t : Set X } ( hs : SizeAtLeastTwo s ) ( ht : SizeAtLeastTwo t ) ( hst : ¬ SizeAtLeastTwo ( s ∩ t )) : SizeAtLeastThree ( s ∪ t ) := by sorry
html:
• Let us define that a set \(s\) in a type \(X\) has size at least two, if there exist \(x_1\) and \(x_2\) in \(s\) which are different, and has size at least three, if there exist \(x_1\), \(x_2\) and \(x_3\) in \(s\) which are all different.

    Let \(s\) and \(t\) be sets of size at least two in some type \(X\), and suppose that \(s \cap t\) does not have size at least two. Show that \(s\cup t\) has size at least three.

    This problem features a ton of different cases. Use exhaust liberally to knock off sub-cases.

    def SizeAtLeastTwo (s : Set X) : Prop := ∃ x1 x2 : X, x1 ≠ x2 ∧ x1 ∈ s ∧ x2 ∈ s
def SizeAtLeastThree (s : Set X) : Prop :=
  ∃ x1 x2 x3 : X, x1 ≠ x2 ∧ x1 ≠ x3 ∧ x2 ≠ x3 ∧ x1 ∈ s ∧ x2 ∈ s ∧ x3 ∈ s

example {s t : Set X} (hs : SizeAtLeastTwo s) (ht : SizeAtLeastTwo t)
    (hst : ¬ SizeAtLeastTwo (s ∩ t)) :
    SizeAtLeastThree (s ∪ t) := by
  sorry
zh: 我们定义：类型 \(X\) 中的集合 \(s\) 的大小至少为二，若存在 \(s\) 中两个不同元素 \(x_1\) 和 \(x_2\)；大小至少为三，若存在 \(s\) 中三个两两不同的元素 \(x_1,x_2,x_3\)。设 \(s\) 和 \(t\) 是某个类型 \(X\) 中大小至少为二的集合，并假设 \(s\cap t\) 的大小并非至少为二。证明 \(s\cup t\) 的大小至少为三。本题有大量不同情形；请大量使用 exhaust 来清除子情形。

text: Consider the sequence \(U_n\) of sets of integers defined recursively by, \[\begin{split}U_0&=\mathbb{Z} \\ \text{for }n:\mathbb{N},\quad U_{n+1} &=\{x:\mathbb{Z}\mid \exists \ y\in U_n, x = 2y \}\end{split}\] Show that for all natural numbers \(n\) , \(U_n=\{x:\mathbb{Z}\mid 2^n\mid x\}\) . def U : ℕ → Set ℤ | 0 => univ | n + 1 => { x : ℤ | ∃ y ∈ U n , x = 2 * y } example ( n : ℕ ) : U n = { x : ℤ | ( 2 : ℤ ) ^ n ∣ x } := by simple_induction n with k hk · rw [ U ] sorry · rw [ U ] ext x dsimp sorry
html:
• Consider the sequence \(U_n\) of sets of integers defined recursively by,

    \[\begin{split}U_0&=\mathbb{Z} \\ \text{for }n:\mathbb{N},\quad U_{n+1} &=\{x:\mathbb{Z}\mid \exists \ y\in U_n, x = 2y \}\end{split}\]

    Show that for all natural numbers \(n\), \(U_n=\{x:\mathbb{Z}\mid 2^n\mid x\}\).

    def U : ℕ → Set ℤ
  | 0 => univ
  | n + 1 => {x : ℤ | ∃ y ∈ U n, x = 2 * y}

example (n : ℕ) : U n = {x : ℤ | (2:ℤ) ^ n ∣ x} := by
  simple_induction n with k hk
  · rw [U]
    sorry
  · rw [U]
    ext x
    dsimp
    sorry
zh: 考虑按如下递归定义的整数集合序列 \(U_n\)：\[\begin{split}U_0&=\mathbb{Z} \\ \text{for }n:\mathbb{N},\quad U_{n+1} &=\{x:\mathbb{Z}\mid \exists y\in U_n, x = 2y \}\end{split}\]证明对所有自然数 \(n\)，都有 \(U_n=\{x:\mathbb{Z}\mid 2^n\mid x\}\)。
